board_index_struct.brief: A struct that encapsulates the core properties and identifiers of a message board within the system.
board_index_struct.details: The `board_index_struct` serves as a comprehensive container for the essential attributes of a message board. It includes `board_hdr`, a constant string representing the header or title of the board, which is used for display purposes. The `board_list` is a pointer to an array of `Note` pointers, managing the collection of messages or notes associated with the board, allowing dynamic handling of varying message counts. The `board_short` provides a concise, standardized identifier for the board, facilitating quick references and internal processing. The `board_plural` offers the pluralized form of the board's name, supporting grammatically correct UI and messaging outputs. Lastly, `board_long` contains a full, descriptive name of the board, suitable for detailed display or documentation. Together, these members enable organized, flexible management of message boards, supporting features such as display, referencing, and message handling within the system.
board_index_struct.notes: This struct is designed following a data encapsulation pattern, grouping related properties of a message board to facilitate easy management and access. The use of constant strings for identifiers ensures immutability where appropriate, and the pointer to `Note` objects allows dynamic message collection handling.
board_index_struct.rationale: The `board_index_struct` exists to provide a unified, organized way to represent message board metadata and content references within the messaging subsystem. It supports the system's architecture by enabling efficient display, referencing, and management of multiple message boards, thereby maintaining clarity and consistency across user interfaces and internal processes.
board_index_struct.name: board_index_struct

board_hdr.brief: A string that stores the header or title of a specific board.
board_hdr.details: The `board_hdr` variable holds the header or title associated with a particular board within the application. It is initialized with a `String` object, which provides enhanced string manipulation features beyond standard strings, such as case-insensitive comparisons. This header is primarily used in user-facing messages and display contexts to clearly identify the source or context of notes or messages related to the board, facilitating user recognition and interaction.
board_hdr.notes: Since `board_hdr` is a `const` member, it is set during initialization and remains unchanged throughout the lifetime of the object, ensuring consistent identification of the board.
board_hdr.rationale: This variable exists to provide a readily accessible, immutable label for each board, supporting clear and consistent communication in user interfaces and message outputs.
board_hdr.name: board_hdr

usages._note_8cc, void do_next(Character *ch, S: Used in the output message header to identify the board from which the note is taken.

board_list.brief: A pointer to an array of Note pointers representing the collection of notes on a specific message board.
board_list.details: The `board_list` variable is a double pointer to Note objects, acting as the primary container for all notes associated with a particular message board. It is usually initialized to point to a dynamically allocated array of Note pointers, which can be resized or modified as notes are added or removed. This structure enables efficient management and access to the notes within the board, supporting operations such as listing, insertion, and deletion of notes throughout the messaging system.
board_list.notes: Since `board_list` is a raw pointer to an array, careful memory management is required to prevent leaks or dangling pointers. It is common to allocate and deallocate this array dynamically, and to keep track of its size separately.
board_list.rationale: This variable exists to maintain a flexible and dynamic collection of notes for each message board, allowing the system to efficiently handle varying numbers of notes and support core messaging functionalities.
board_list.name: board_list

board_short.brief: A constant string storing the abbreviated name or identifier of the board.
board_short.details: The `board_short` variable holds a concise, human-readable abbreviation for the board, used primarily for display purposes in user interfaces, logs, or reports. It is initialized during the construction of a `board_index_struct` instance and remains immutable afterward, ensuring consistent identification. Utilizing the custom `String` class, it benefits from enhanced string operations, including case-insensitive comparisons, which facilitate flexible matching and display functionalities related to the board's short name.
board_short.notes: Since `board_short` is declared as `const`, it is set once during initialization and cannot be modified later, ensuring its value remains stable throughout the object's lifetime.
board_short.rationale: This variable exists to provide a standardized, easily accessible short identifier for the board, supporting consistent referencing and display across various components of the system.
board_short.name: board_short

board_plural.brief: Stores the plural form of the board's name for display and messaging purposes.
board_plural.details: The `board_plural` variable holds the pluralized version of the board's name, which is used across the application for user interface labels, messages, and other display elements that refer to multiple boards. It is initialized from the singular name, typically during object construction or setup routines, ensuring consistency in terminology. As a `String` object with extended features, it allows for flexible string operations, including case-insensitive comparisons, which can be useful for localization or user input handling.
board_plural.notes: Being a constant, `board_plural` is set once during initialization and remains unchanged, ensuring stability in display references throughout the program.
board_plural.rationale: This variable exists to provide a readily available, correctly pluralized form of the board's name, supporting clear and consistent communication in the user interface and related functionalities.
board_plural.name: board_plural

board_long.brief: Holds the full descriptive name or label of the board as a string.
board_long.details: The `board_long` variable contains a human-readable, descriptive name for the board, which can be used for display purposes or detailed identification. It is initialized with a `String` object that extends standard string functionality, potentially supporting case-insensitive comparisons and advanced text manipulation. This variable is referenced in various parts of the application where a comprehensive board name is required, ensuring consistent labeling across the system.
board_long.notes: Since `board_long` is a constant, it is set during initialization and remains unchanged throughout the program's execution, ensuring stable identification labels.
board_long.rationale: This variable exists to provide a clear, descriptive label for the board that can be used in user interfaces, logs, or reports, supporting better readability and user understanding.
board_long.name: board_long
